subject: Run in Python 3.8
commit_message:
Run in Python 3.8

Use Docker image prepared by integration team

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2292

Signed-off-by: Michal Jagiello <michal.jagiello@t-mobile.pl>
Change-Id: I6dda392465a20f7199cf3a27a9835c692bf7aaf4

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Vijay, it looks that here we should also install Python 3.8 for CI

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hi Vijay, it looks that here we should also install Python 3.8 for CI

Hi Michal -  The error is reg module not found and not a build/CI node issue (ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g has 3.8 already installed and used for other components)
Removed reviewer Tony Hansen.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Hi Vijay, it looks that here we should also install Python 3.8 for CI
> 
> Hi Michal -  The error is reg module not found and not a build/CI node issue (ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g has 3.8 already installed and used for other components)

That's true, it's because tox runs tests using Python 2.7 somehow instead of Python 3.8. I thought that Python 3.8 is not installed and that's the reason why it uses the wrong version

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > Hi Vijay, it looks that here we should also install Python 3.8 for CI
> > 
> > Hi Michal -  The error is reg module not found and not a build/CI node issue (ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g has 3.8 already installed and used for other components)
> 
> That's true, it's because tox runs tests using Python 2.7 somehow instead of Python 3.8. I thought that Python 3.8 is not installed and that's the reason why it uses the wrong version

I see in logs that during build tox==2.9.1 version is installed, what is pretty old one

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 1:
> > > > 
> > > > Hi Vijay, it looks that here we should also install Python 3.8 for CI
> > > 
> > > Hi Michal -  The error is reg module not found and not a build/CI node issue (ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g has 3.8 already installed and used for other components)
> > 
> > That's true, it's because tox runs tests using Python 2.7 somehow instead of Python 3.8. I thought that Python 3.8 is not installed and that's the reason why it uses the wrong version
> 
> I see in logs that during build tox==2.9.1 version is installed, what is pretty old one

Looks like that is coming from https://git.onap.org/dcaegen2/utils/tree/scripts/mvn-phase-lib.sh#n238 (which gets pushed into nexus/raw and used in PH build)

1) Switch Policy Handler to use global python jobs (and remove mvn execution scripts) - this is similar to CBS setup
2) Update the script in dcaegen2/utils repo and push to raw and then recheck Policy-handler

I'm good with either, although Option #1 would be preferered. Michal - Can you support with the required changes?
